description:
**Narrowed scope** (CD-bundle handles the recursive cons-in-tail case via TRMC).

When a `MakeList` appears with a known-length, non-recursive shape (`[a, b, c]`), and the surrounding cont's dst is `List`: pre-allocate the entire cons chain in one bumped span and store cells contiguously.

**Why narrowed**: the recursive case `[h | recurse(t)]` is the canonical TRMC pattern and was handled atomically with the wire ABI in fz-q9g.3 (CD-bundle). Literals are a distinct, additive optimization.

**Still BLOCKED BY fz-6rw** because contiguous span needs the 16-byte cell layout to actually win against the bump-per-cell baseline. If RC for cons proves infeasible at 16B, close this ticket — recursive cons-in-tail (the primary FBIP target) is already covered.

**Tests**: golden CLIF on `[a, b, c]` construction; demonstrate single bump-region call instead of per-cell.
title: fz-dps.F4: DPS for known-length MakeList literals
